methods.description: This method involves estimating the total number of individuals of each species in a pit by tallying specific skeletal elements. The most frequently preserved element is used to estimate the total number of individuals, providing a dependable count of preserved individuals.

steps.description: For each species in each pit, a tally was made of one skeletal element, for example, the right calcaneum.
steps.searchable_summary: tally skeletal elements for each species in each pit

steps.description: This count was repeated for all other elements for which there seemed to have been a good chance of preservation and collection and a small chance of loss or breakage in accumulation.
steps.searchable_summary: repeat tally for all well-preserved elements

steps.description: The tallies for the separate elements, or bones, were reviewed and the total number of individuals in a pit was estimated from the number of specimens of the most frequently preserved element in that pit.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate total individuals from most frequent element

steps.description: Such inconsistencies between different elements of a species within a pit were noted in several cases and may, perhaps, be accounted for by incomplete preservation of individuals or by breakage or loss in collection.

methods.description: This method compiles data from museum catalogues to create a census of species in various pits. It involves using both permanent ledgers and older card catalogues to gather information on species identification, skeletal elements, and pit numbers.

steps.description: About half of the species of large Rancho La Brea mammals are catalogued in a series of permanent ledgers, which indicate catalogue number, species identification, skeletal element, pit number and coordinates within pit for each specimen.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch species data from permanent ledgers

steps.description: Some of the rarer large mammals are catalogued in an older, card-catalogue. Both catalogues were used to make the pit censuses.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch species data from card catalogues

steps.description: A direct count of bones was made only for Bison, which is incompletely catalogued, and for Camelops as a verification of the catalogue.
steps.searchable_summary: perform direct bone count for Bison and Camelops
